author: l.tran@bitterrootrehab.com
body: Great update to the session format. The new pacing module in week 3 is working well based on feedback.

author: b.rivers@bitterrootrehab.com
body: Can we add a version without the sleep section for participants who are already working with Sandra on that?

author: f.schultz@bitterrootrehab.com
body: Good point - I'll create a short form version by end of June.

author: j.park@bitterrootrehab.com
body: I've completed the week 12 competency items. Ready for the mid-point review meeting.

author: f.schultz@bitterrootrehab.com
body: Confirmed - mid-point review scheduled for Apr 22 at 10am. Jasmine is doing excellent work on the pacing group documentation.

author: k.tanaka@bitterrootrehab.com
body: The hand splinting section of the study guide was really helpful. Sharing with Sarah too.

author: t.beaumont@bitterrootrehab.com
body: trainer side updated - added the PSFS outcome measure integration in section 4.

author: h.kline@bitterrootrehab.com
body: SLP added communication strategy tips for participants with pain-related anxiety - see section 6.

author: f.schultz@bitterrootrehab.com
body: Starting to add pre-reading notes. Will update this page after the Nov 14-16 intensive with takeaways.

author: j.torres@bitterrootrehab.com
body: I updated the goal-setting section with references from the wrist rehab population - hope that's helpful.

author: b.rivers@bitterrootrehab.com
body: Added my CEU hours from the Seattle occupational health seminar. Are we sharing this log?

author: f.schultz@bitterrootrehab.com
body: Yes, feel free to use it - it's shared with all Occupational Therapy staff. Just add your own section.

author: l.tran@bitterrootrehab.com
body: Excellent summary Frank. This supports the project for our expanded pain group offering in 2027.

author: f.schultz@bitterrootrehab.com
body: Confirmed my 2026 privacy annual training completion. No changes to Occupational Therapy documentation workflow needed.

author: l.tran@bitterrootrehab.com
body: Looking strong Frank - especially the HTCC goal and the student supervision initiative. Let's review at your Q3 check-in.

author: j.torres@bitterrootrehab.com
body: The module 3 slides on sensitization were a great addition. Readers respond really well to the metaphors.

author: s.okafor@bitterrootrehab.com
body: The sleep section is very practical. I'm referencing this when participants ask about rest vs. activity balance.

author: j.park@bitterrootrehab.com
body: Just finished the onboarding guide - super helpful to understand the documentation culture here.

author: k.tanaka@bitterrootrehab.com
body: Added a note about thermoplastic softening temperature variation for the Orfit Classic material.

author: f.schultz@bitterrootrehab.com
body: Updated week 6 to include the outdoor pacing application content - connects well with our participant population in Missoula.
